messages.content: The problem is that if a player from the second TrainingArea goes to the same corner that has food in the first TrainingArea, then the food will disappear in the first TrainingArea.
Great, now players from other TrainingAreas can't eat food from the first TrainingArea. The problem remains that they cannot eat food from their TrainingArea.

you did not understand me. even if there is only one TrainingArea left on the stage, and if I move it to the right, my food will be considered eaten if it is far to the right. The same for the left, the more I move the TrainingArea to the left, the more it will be on the left, even if the sprites are displayed correctly. The same goes for top and bottom. The ideal position of the TrainingArea is 0,0,0 where everything works correctly. Fix it.
Assets\PlayerAgent.cs(30,9): error CS0246: The type or namespace name 'List<>' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

Apologies, I forgot to add the required `System.Collections.Generic` namespace that includes the `List<>` class.

Please add the following using directive at the top of your PlayerAgent.cs script:

```csharp
using System.Collections.Generic;
```

Your script should now compile without any issues. Run your project to test if everything works as expected. If you face any issues, please let me know, and I'll be glad to help.
